signal: Agent ends without calling publish-session after long build-structure or content runs (safety-net fired 3/5 ticks in G1); Step 6 in architect.agent.md lacked emphatic terminal language
expected_impact: -2 to -3 safety-net entries per generation; prevents premature replanting

signal: Agent ran plan-roadmap (19 turns) then stopped without proceeding to Execute, producing a safety-net tick with only a ROADMAP.md change and no content
expected_impact: -1 partial wasted run per generation; prevents roadmap-only safety-net ticks

observed_in: G1 run 10 (check-lifecycle saw 4 ticks including 2 safety-nets → replanted at tick 4 vs 3 real ticks)
signal: (SUPERSEDED by L004 — analysis was wrong direction) Originally believed safety-net entries inflated the counter; actually they were invisible to it

Safety-net §8 entries titled 'Growth tick (safety net)' were invisible to check-lifecycle's 'Tick N:' counter — generation_ticks stalled at 2/3, replant never fired. L003 had the wrong direction (it said entries inflated; actually they didn't count at all).
expected_impact: prevents 'why won't it replant' re-investigation; stall diagnostic embedded in step 2 so future operators know exactly where to look

observed_in: 1776-G1 runs 6,7 (plan-roadmap early-stop, same class as L002 but gap in architect.agent.md not grow.prompt.md)
signal: architect.agent.md step 2 lacked an explicit 'proceed to step 3 immediately' gate; L002 embedded the gate in grow.prompt.md but not in the Architect agent itself, leaving a gap when the architect is invoked directly
expected_impact: closes the step-2 early-stop gap for direct Architect invocations; reduces plan-only wasted runs

signal: Architect's step-0 'If dormant, report and stop' left shepherd mode completely undocumented; agent had to re-derive the full procedure (clone remote repo, run curator+build+sync+publish manually) from scratch each mature-repo tick
expected_impact: -3 to -5 turns per shepherd tick; prevents fragile ad-hoc re-derivation of the clone-and-grow procedure

signal: check-lifecycle always re-reads seed.md §8 even when lifecycle.yml.state.status=mature (generation finalized); run 12 confirmed 'generation_ticks (5) already matches — no write-back needed' after a redundant recount
expected_impact: -2 tool calls per shepherd tick on mature repos (skip §8 read + grep)

signal: Run #13 hit subscription session limit at the very first turn ('You've hit your session limit') — 618s wall-clock, 0 tool calls, fully wasted compute slot; no prompt-layer fix possible since Claude cannot read prompts when rate-limited
expected_impact: a grow.yml pre-flight check (e.g. --output-limit or timeout guard) could detect a zero-tool session and mark it as rate-limited rather than success
